topic: GPT-5.3 Codex Spark Review 2026: OpenAI Coding Model Benchmarked

key_points: 1,000+ tokens/sec on Cerebras WSE-3 hardware — 15x faster than standard Codex
SWE-Bench Pro score ~56% vs Codex 5.3's ~72%
Best for rapid prototyping, targeted edits, iterative frontend loops
Weak on multi-step architecture and stateful debugging
Research preview for ChatGPT Pro users only at launch
Speed vs. intelligence trade-off is the central tension
Codex 5.3 took 6 minutes vs Spark's 50 seconds on the same task
Recommended two-model workflow: Spark for iterations, Codex 5.3 for hard reasoning
128k context window vs Codex 5.3's 192k context
Frontend devs and indie hackers gravitated toward Spark for small, self-contained edits
1,000 TPS eliminates the AI 'thinking loop' latency problem
Real-time collaboration: interrupt and redirect while model works
One X user built a full SpriteKit game in 20 minutes
Reduces round-trip overhead by 80% via persistent WebSocket
Time-to-first-token improved by 50%
WSE-3 chip has 4 trillion transistors on single wafer — eliminates GPU cluster bottlenecks
44GB SRAM directly on chip eliminates off-chip memory latency
OpenAI-Cerebras partnership announced January 2026, worth $10B+
10-20x speed gains over Nvidia H100 clusters
Available through Codex app, CLI, and VS Code extension
Terminal-Bench 2.0: GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark scores 77.3% (up from GPT-5.2's 64%)
SWE-Lancer IC Diamond: Codex 5.3 scores 81.4%
OSWorld-Verified: Codex 5.3 scores 64.7%
Spark struggles with security-critical code, auth, encryption, input validation
JSON schema and tool-call formatting reliability issues flagged by developers

stats.fact: GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark delivers over 1,000 tokens per second on Cerebras WSE-3 hardware — 15x faster than standard GPT-5.3-Codex
Codex Spark scores ~56% on SWE-Bench Pro, compared to GPT-5.3-Codex's ~72%
OpenAI reduced per-token overhead by 30% and time-to-first-token by 50% via WebSocket infrastructure improvements
Cerebras WSE-3 chip contains 4 trillion transistors and 44GB of on-chip SRAM on a single wafer
GPT-5.3-Codex API pricing: $1.75/M input tokens, $14/M output tokens (updated April 2, 2026 to token-based pricing)
Codex Spark requires ChatGPT Pro ($200/month) subscription during research preview; not yet available via API
OpenAI-Cerebras partnership (announced January 2026) valued at over $10 billion
128k context window vs GPT-5.3-Codex's 192k context at launch (text-only)
Real-world test: Spark completed a task in 50 seconds vs Codex 5.3's 6 minutes (7x faster)
